title: crusea hispida subsp. grandiflora
intro: Crusea hispida subsp. grandiflora, also known as the Large-flowered Crusea or Large-flowered Crusea Hairy Crusea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family, Gentianaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Crusea hispida subsp. grandiflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers and then steeping them in hot water or mixing them with a solvent to create a liquid extract.
